Title: DETACH
Description: Makes the server "forget" about the existence of a table, a materialized view, a dictionary, or a database.

Detaching does not delete the data or metadata of a table, a materialized view, a dictionary or a database. If an entity was not detachedPERMANENTLY, on the next server launch the server will read the metadata and recall the table/view/dictionary/database again. If an entity was detachedPERMANENTLY, there will be no automatic recall.

Whether a table, a dictionary or a database was detached permanently or not, in both cases you can reattach them using theATTACHquery.
System log tables can be also attached back (e.g.query_log,text_log, etc.). Other system tables can't be reattached. On the next server launch the server will recall those tables again.
ATTACH MATERIALIZED VIEWdoes not work with short syntax (withoutSELECT), but you can attach it using theATTACH TABLEquery.
Note that you can not detach permanently the table which is already detached (temporary). But you can attach it back and then detach permanently again.
Also, you can notDROPthe detached table, orCREATE TABLEwith the same name as detached permanently, or replace it with the other table withRENAME TABLEquery.
TheSYNCmodifier executes the action without delay.
In ClickHouse Cloud users should use thePERMANENTLYclause e.g.DETACH TABLE <table> PERMANENTLY. If this clause is not used, tables will be reattached on cluster restart e.g. during upgrades.
